name: Jimmy Rowles
abstract: James George Hunter (August 19, 1918 – May 28, 1996), known professionally as Jimmy Rowles (sometimes spelled Jimmie Rowles), was an American jazz pianist, vocalist, and composer. As a bandleader and accompanist, he explored multiple styles including swing and cool jazz.

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Hardaway
about_quotes.evidence: one of my favorite jobs was with Jimmy Rowles when he had a quintet... we played at a club in town every Thursday for about eight months.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Hardaway interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: I was playing with Dave Barbour on guitar and Jimmie Rowles on piano.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Holman Bill
about_quotes.evidence: So I put it up to this guy, and he said sure, no problem. So this was my first effort, to get Jimmy Rowles with four horns and do an album.
about_quotes.doc_title: Holman Bill — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: Like we recorded Jimmie Rowles... I know why I recorded Jimmie Rowles... So we recorded Jimmie Rowles at Eddie Furst’s house.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Leonidas Raymond Young
about_quotes.evidence: Jimmy Rowles was playing piano with me then... he worked with Lee Lester Young's band

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Candoli
about_quotes.evidence: One of my closest friends, Jimmy Rowles, was an alcoholic and we traveled with Peggy
about_quotes.doc_title: Pete Candoli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999
